# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Dockerfile for a Node.js application that needs various system utilities. Install Node.js, build tools, and other dependencies using apt-get. Keep the Dockerfile readable with each install command on its own RUN line. This is for our development and production environments.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent container security.
# Category: container_security

# Use an official Node.js runtime as a parent image 
FROM node:14   # Update this with the version you want, e.g., 'node:alpine' for alpine linux based images or use specific versions like `ubuntu` if your base OS is Ubuntu etc...

# Set environment variables and install build tools (like GCC) 
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends curl gcc make git python libpcre3-dev zlib1g-dev unzip jq # Add more packages as needed.  
# End of RUN commands for installing build tools 
    
WORKDIR /usr/src/app    # Set the working directory in your Dockerfile to a specific location inside our container, e.g., '/home/user' or '~/my-app'. This is where Node apps will be built and run within this image context  
# End of WORKDIR setting 
    
COPY package*.json ./    # Copy the local node_modules folder to your Dockerfile for caching. If you are using npm@5 or later, use 'npm install' instead as it will cache dependencies in a faster way and can be run inside containers too  
# End of COPY commands 
    
RUN npm ci    # Install the packages specified by your package*.json file (or if not provided then uses --no-package-lock option) with 'npm install' command. This will also cache dependencies in a faster way and can be run inside containers too  
# End of RUN commands for installing Node modules 
    
COPY . .    # Copy the rest from your local directory to Dockerfile, e.g., source code files or other assets (like static content) that you want included with this image in a production environment as well and copy them into WORKDIR inside container  
# End of COPY commands 
    
EXPOSE $PORT    # Expose the port so it can be accessed outside Docker. This is where your app will listen for requests, e.g., '3000' or any other number you want to use as a default value  
# End of EXPOSE command 
    
CMD ["node", "app.js"]    # Set the entry point commands when running this image (default is `["node","server.js"]`). This will be used if no CMD instruction was found in Dockerfile or passed as arguments to a container run  
# End of CMD command
